sources.purpose: Gap analysis — current ChildDispatchRecord and LoopState schema
Session field definitions, attachment capability model, gap analysis baseline

design_decisions.decision: Feature flags are additive and independent
design_decisions.rationale: Each Connect feature can be enabled without enabling others; no ordering dependency is enforced at the flag level, but a recommended enablement order is documented

design_decisions.decision: attachment_capable is a stored boolean, not inferred from provider
design_decisions.rationale: Inferring capability from provider name at Connect query time is fragile; the adapter writes the authoritative value at dispatch time

design_decisions.decision: session_id is top-level on ChildDispatchRecord, not nested under WorkerAssignmentRecord
design_decisions.rationale: worker_assignment.subagent_session_id is provider-specific and buried; a top-level session_id gives Connect a single field lookup regardless of dispatch mode

design_decisions.decision: Connect does not write to LoopState directly
design_decisions.rationale: State mutations go through Polaris API to maintain single-writer invariant; Connect is a read-mostly system with narrow write paths (interrupt/takeover events via API)

design_decisions.decision: Operator audit events (operator-interrupt, operator-takeover) are deferred to Connect enablement
design_decisions.rationale: These events require Connect to be implemented; their schemas are documented here for planning but the appendAuditEvent function is out of scope for POL-216
